Issue: Enhance ExtractDataKeyFromMetaKeyd to work with MetaTensor objects
Decision: Add __contains__ and __getitem__ to MetaTensor to enable string-key access into its internal meta
Rationale: The trade-off is that it touches the core MetaTensor class (wider blast radius), whereas this PR's approach is scoped to a single transform. But for long-term maintainability the __contains__/__getitem__ approach is better since it avoids per-transform isinstance(meta_obj, MetaTensor) checks everywhere.
Author: haoyu-haoyu
Risk: Adding __getitem__ to MetaTensor causes infinite recursion in torch.as_tensor() — PyTorch internally calls __len__ and __getitem__ on inputs, which triggers __torch_function__ dispatch, creating a cycle

Justification: The decision involves modifying the MetaTensor class (core implementation) by adding __contains__ and __getitem__ methods, which are related to model/data structure changes. The discussion also references trade-offs in maintainability and blast radius, typical of modeling/development decisions. Keywords like 'layers' (implied in core class modifications) and the focus on algorithmic/structural changes support this classification.
